title: See below in press release for additional information.

raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "Senate Democrats passed a budget that did not address affordability issues.",
    "Legislative Republicans offered an alternative budget proposal to meet the needs of Californians.",
    "Senator Strickland criticized the Democrats for funding the controversial ‘train to nowhere.’",
    "Senate Democrats allocated only $110 million for the anti-crime initiative, which Senator Strickland claims barely addresses the issue.",
    "There is a lack of funding for the core components of Prop. 36, specifically supervision and accountability.",
    "Legislative Democrats proposed using $2.5 billion of the state reserves and increasing borrowing by $1.5 billion.",
    "The budget proposed by Democrats will worsen ongoing deficits by roughly $6 billion per year.",
    "The budget includes $10.8 billion for Medi-Cal coverage for undocumented immigrants.",
    "The budget contains a $20 million fund to sue the federal government, in addition to a previously allocated $50 million for the same purpose.",
    "$750 million is allocated to bail out the Bay Area Regional Transit.",
    "Legislative Democrats allocated $41 million for the failing High-Speed Rail.",
    "An economic viability study for the High-Speed Rail project will cost $700,000.",
    "The budget allocates $50 million for county behavioral health departments as part of Prop. 36 funding.",
    "The budget includes $30 million for the judicial branch to support workload and initial implementation of Prop. 36.",
    "The budget designates $15 million for the judicial branch for pretrial services under Prop. 36.",
    "The budget allocates $15 million for local public defenders related to Prop. 36.",
    "No additional funds were allocated for local probation departments, which are key to the success of Prop. 36."
  ]
}
```
